primary_accounting_issue: Application of hedge accounting to foreign exchange risk on intragroup dividend receivable in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Establishes that only items with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items, with no direct exception for intragroup items in standard hedge accounting
Provides the specific exception allowing foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements when not fully eliminated on consolidation

supporting_authority.reason: Governs how foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items are treated on consolidation, which determines whether an exposure to foreign exchange gains or losses exists
Addresses disclosure requirements for liabilities arising from financing activities including hedge accounting disclosures
Covers disclosure requirements for consolidated structured entities and group composition relevant to understanding the consolidated reporting entity

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses presentation and disclosure of financial instruments but does not govern hedge accounting treatment for foreign exchange risk
Addresses members' shares in cooperative entities and classification between liability and equity, not directly relevant to foreign exchange hedge accounting on dividends
Addresses measurement and recognition of non-cash dividend payables, not foreign exchange hedge accounting
Addresses classification of income and expenses from derivatives in statement of profit or loss but not hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items
Addresses debt for equity swap transactions, not foreign exchange hedge accounting on dividend receivables
Addresses net investment hedge accounting in foreign operations but for different context

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements
Standard hedge accounting requiring external counterparty

approaches.label: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 intragroup monetary item exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Allows foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items (including dividend receivables) to qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements when the foreign exchange exposure affects consolidated profit or loss and is not fully eliminated on consolidation

approaches.label: Standard IFRS 9 hedge accounting (external party requirement)
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: General hedge accounting requirement under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 that only items with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items; for intragroup items to qualify, they must meet the paragraph 6.3.6 exception conditions
